title: Umami Broil Braise with Bouquet Garni
description: This French-inspired dish combines umami flavors and crispy textures for a crowd-pleasing meal that's easy to prepare.

ingredients.name: Watercress leaves
ingredients.notes: For garnish and to add freshness

ingredients.name: Red wine vinegar
ingredients.notes: For tenderizing the meat and adding acidity

ingredients.name: Brown sugar
ingredients.notes: To add depth of flavor to the marinade

ingredients.name: Bouquet garni
ingredients.notes: A mix of herbs tied in a cheesecloth, used for infusing the liquid

instructions: Marinate pork tenderloin with olive oil, brown sugar, and red wine vinegar for at least 30 minutes.
Coat a broiler pan with olive oil. Place marinated pork on top.
Braise the meat in the preheated oven for about 10-15 minutes until it reaches an internal temperature of 135°F (57°C) or rare, then let rest at room temperature.
While resting, make a reduction by simmering marinade with garlic and lemon zest. Strain into a small bowl.
Preheat broiler to high heat. Brush the surface of rested pork with some of the reduction mixture and sprinkle with brown sugar.
Broil until the skin is golden and crispy, about 2-3 minutes.
Arrange watercress leaves on serving platter, place braise-broiled pork tenderloin on top, drizzle with remaining reduction mixture, and garnish with remaining herb bouquet garni.
